steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank describes what the skeptical colleagues believed about the kea parrot's interactions with the stones. The contrast word "but" signals that the blank must mean the opposite of "deliberately" — the colleagues thought the behavior was unintentional, not planned.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'intriguing': "Intriguing" means fascinating or interesting. Finding the behavior intriguing does not conflict with Bastos's finding that it was deliberate — it describes a reaction, not skepticism about intent. ✗
  • Choice B — 'obvious': "Obvious" means clearly evident. Calling the stone use obvious does not contradict the claim that it was deliberate; someone could find deliberate behavior obvious. No real contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — 'accidental': "Accidental" means unplanned or unintentional — the direct opposite of "deliberately." Skeptics suggesting the interactions were accidental is exactly the doubt that Bastos and her team disproved. ✓
  • Choice D — 'observable': "Observable" means able to be seen or noticed. This does not conflict with deliberate behavior; something can be both observable and deliberate. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
